title: Java String Methods
learningGoals: Type chained String method calls that normalize text before comparing it.
Recall that String methods return new strings instead of mutating the original.

choices: Nothing, because the if condition is false
completionChecks.explanation: raw.trim().toLowerCase() yields "admin@example.com", which is not blank and contains "admin", so the branch prints substring(0, 5) - the first five characters, "admin".

completionChecks.prompt: What does the variable raw refer to after raw.trim().toLowerCase() runs?
choices: "admin@example.com", because trim and toLowerCase modify raw in place
An empty string, because trim removed every character
Compilation fails because String methods cannot be chained
The original "  admin@example.com  ", because String methods return new strings instead of mutating raw
completionChecks.explanation: Strings are immutable, so trim and toLowerCase each return a new String and leave raw unchanged; only the email variable holds the normalized result.

completionChecks.prompt: What does internal hold after email.endsWith("@example.com") runs?
choices: false, because raw still has leading and trailing spaces
Compilation fails because endsWith returns an int
completionChecks.explanation: email is the trimmed, lower-case String, so it ends with @example.com even though raw still keeps its original spaces.
